pr.title: fix(aider-worker): accept aider 0.86 single-line 'Tokens: ... Cost: ...' stdout
pr.body:
Bug C surfaced by the 2026-05-17 v0.4 exit-gate attempt 3
(thread 57f6d9576cc9): aider 0.86.2 prints

    Tokens: 13k sent, 173 received. Cost: $0.07 message, $0.07 session.

— both halves on a SINGLE line. PR #35 (A.3) shipped regexes that
only matched a two-line variant:

    _TOKENS_RE: ... received\.?\\s*$        # requires LINE END after 'received.'
    _COST_RE:   ^\\s*Cost: ...                # requires LINE START before 'Cost:'

Result on the real gate run: both regexes failed, metrics dict was
empty, coder.py's 'if result.metrics' skipped the propagation, the
checkpoint DB had no metrics keys, and 'ai-cockpit cost' reported
'tokens=N/A cost=N/A coverage=0/8'. Q4(1) <= $1 became
unfalsifiable: the gate could never produce its own cost evidence.

The PR #35 unit tests all used multi-line fixtures
('Tokens: ... received.\\nCost: ...'), so CI stayed green even
though no real aider invocation ever round-tripped through the
regex. docs/V0_3_MILESTONES.md 'Cost / token signal' actually
quoted the correct single-line form verbatim
('Tokens: 11k sent, 566 received. Cost: $0.07.'), but the
implementation drifted from the milestone notes.

Fix:

- aider_worker.py: drop the tail '\\s*$' anchor from _TOKENS_RE so
  the Tokens half can be followed by ' Cost: ...' on the same line;
  drop the head '^\\s*' anchor from _COST_RE so 'Cost:' can appear
  anywhere on a line (preceded by 'received.' or on its own line).
  Both variants — single-line AND legacy two-line — now match.
  Inline comment cites the 2026-05-17 gate as the surfacing event
  and explains why the anchors were dropped.

- test_aider_worker.py: add 3 regression fixtures using the real
  single-line aider 0.86.2 output captured from the gate run:
  (1) one-turn single-line, (2) multi-turn single-line (last-turn
  values win, mirroring the existing multi-line last-wins test),
  (3) single-line with leading indent (matches the actual indented
  form inside ai-cockpit's Run Summary block).

All existing 6 parametrized fixtures + 4 silent-fallback fixtures
still pass — backward compatibility preserved.

Out of scope:

- aider 0.87+ output format changes (none observed yet; the fix
  remains version-agnostic).
- WorkerResult.metrics schema (unchanged: same four keys).
- cost.py aggregator (unchanged: same keys read from checkpoint).
- B.5 contract / scripts/v0_4_exit_gate.sh (Bug A and Bug B follow
  in separate PRs; this PR is the smallest of the three and
  unblocks Q4(1) evidence regardless of the other two).

Bugs A (test_demo_fixture guard) and B (planner test-command path)
will land as PR-after-this. They surfaced together on the same gate
attempt but are independent fixes.

Pre-push: pytest 288 passed (was 285; +3 new aider fixtures), ruff
clean, mypy clean (78 source files), smoke command prints summary.

Co-authored-by: nanyang12138 <nanyang12138@users.noreply.github.com>
